summary: Excellent test coverage with 66 tests across 3 test files. All 5 TDD anchors are implemented and pass. Tests are fast (under 1 second total), use meaningful assertions, and cover both happy paths and edge cases. FakeTimeProvider is a clean testability pattern.

findings.finding: All 5 TDD anchors verified: testSessionProposal (3 tests), testSessionExpiry (4 tests), testSessionPersistence (3 tests), testRelayValidation (5 tests), testInvalidQrUri (5 tests). Plus 13 additional lifecycle/error/init tests.

21 parser tests cover: valid URI, relay URL variants, whitespace, wrong scheme, empty string, v1 URI, missing params, missing version separator, empty topic, non-numeric version, untrusted relay, quick-check method, and allowed relays set.
12 serializer tests cover: single/multiple sessions roundtrip, empty list, empty/null/blank/invalid JSON, null icon, empty chains, missing optional fields, and timestamp precision.
Mock strategy is appropriate: SecureStorage mocked with in-memory backing (1 mock), TimeProvider faked. Only 1 real mock dependency, well within the 3-mock guideline.

tdd_anchor_coverage.testRelayValidation: PASS - 5 tests: walletconnectCom_allowed, walletconnectOrg_allowed, customRelay_rejected, maliciousRelay_rejected, proposalWithUntrustedRelay_rejected
tdd_anchor_coverage.testInvalidQrUri: PASS - 5 tests: randomText, httpUrl, wcV1Uri, emptyString, wcWithoutRequiredParams

passed_checks: All 5 TDD anchors implemented and passing
Tests cover requirements and edge cases
Tests are independent and isolated (setUp clears state)
